organization.name: Toyota Motor Corporation
keywords: automobile and light duty motor vehicle manufacturing
organization.raw_address: 1 Toyota-Cho, Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture 471-8571, JP
organization.short_description:
Toyota Motor Corporation is a leading Japanese automotive manufacturer, established in 1937. It has grown to become the world's largest automobile manufacturer by volume. The company originated from Toyoda Automatic Loom Works and launched its first passenger car, the Model AA, in 1936. Over the years, Toyota has introduced several iconic models, including the Corolla, which is recognized as the world's best-selling car.

Toyota produces a diverse range of vehicles, focusing on reliability, fuel efficiency, and innovation. Its lineup includes popular passenger cars like the Corolla and Prius, as well as SUVs and trucks such as the Land Cruiser and Tundra. The company also offers luxury vehicles under the Lexus brand, which was launched in 1989. Toyota operates manufacturing plants globally, including in the U.S., Europe, and Asia, and provides financial services through its subsidiary, Toyota Motor Credit Corp. The company serves a wide array of markets, targeting individual consumers, fleets, and motorsport enthusiasts across more than 140 countries.
